title: Simpler — Internal Filter
summary: Simpler's built-in filter shapes the tone of a sample without needing a separate EQ or AutoFilter. Each Simpler instance has its own filter, controllable per drum pad.
steps: Open a Simpler (inside a Drum Rack pad, or standalone)
In the Classic tab, find the Filter section and click the power button to enable it
Choose filter type: LP (lowpass) = cuts highs, leaves lows. HP (highpass) = cuts lows, leaves highs. BP (bandpass) = cuts both extremes, passes a band
Freq knob: sets the cutoff frequency. LP at 8kHz = slightly dark. LP at 3kHz = dark. HP at 200Hz = removes rumble from a pad sample
Res knob: resonance at the cutoff point. 0% = smooth cutoff. 30–50% = the classic synth filter 'peak' sound
The filter can be modulated by the Simpler's Envelope (Env) or LFO (LFO) — set the mod destination amount below the Freq knob
tips: Use HP on a pad sample loaded into Simpler to remove low-end rumble — cleaner than adding EQ Eight as a separate device
A slight LP roll-off (Freq 12kHz, no resonance) on a hi-hat sample makes it sit further back in the mix without being obviously filtered
The Simpler filter is less precise than an EQ Eight — for surgical frequency work, use EQ Eight. For general tone shaping, the Simpler filter is faster
Resonance above 60% on LP creates an aggressive, self-oscillating filter peak — use intentionally for acid bass effects
practice_hint: In rep-01 or any drum rep, open a clap Simpler, enable LP filter, set Freq to 8kHz. Compare with filter off. Then try HP at 150Hz on the same clap — hear how it changes the character.
ableton_version_note: Available in Simpler in Ableton Live Standard and Suite.
